text: Predominantly in laboratory mammal studies, malformations from toxicant exposure or genetic damage have resulted in a variety of types of spermatogenesis impairment.  Abnormalities in epididymes disrupt the connection to testes and proper organ formation, inhibiting development of sperm with proper fertility, maturation, and reproductive capabilities, as well as proper storage.  Empirical studies generally examine sperm counts, the number of abnormal sperm, and whether germ cell formation is limited.
